methods.name: Stage-Progression
methods.rationale: Applies typical Series A → next-stage step-up multiplier (2.0x) to $25M total raised proxy.

methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.rationale: Median across 3 same-sector + same-stage peers in Defense & Security. Range: $150M - $500M.

data.narrative:
Sagar Defence Engineering is a Series A-stage defense and security company that has raised $25.4M to date. The valuation analysis draws on a comparable universe of two public companies (DRSHF, IDEAFORGE.NS) and eight private peers, with direct stage and sector alignment to three primary comparables: Stark Defence ($500M), Vatn Systems ($300M), and Ares Industries ($150M).

Two triangulation methods were applied. The stage-progression method uses total capital raised as a valuation proxy and applies a 2.0x step-up multiplier typical of Series A advancement, yielding $51M. The peer-median method anchors to the median valuation of three same-stage, same-sector comparables, producing $300M; the peer range spans $150M to $500M. The divergence between methods reflects uncertainty around whether the company's metrics and competitive position track closer to the lower or upper quartile of the peer set.

The triangulated analysis yields a range of $43M (low), $175M (median), to $345M (high). The wide dispersion—driven principally by the spread between stage-progression and peer-median approaches—indicates material uncertainty regarding normalized valuation multiples within the Defense & Security frontier-tech subset. Key unknowns include customer concentration, revenue trajectory, IP defensibility, and the extent to which peer valuations reflect genuine product-market traction or sector enthusiasm. Further commercial diligence would be required to narrow this range.
